The blackberry, cherry, cassis and chocolate flavors have all kind of overtones, ranging from licorice and vanilla to gingerbread and candied violets. Saxum has become a cult favorite, one of the highest-priced brands from Paso Robles, and wines like this are the reason why. Drink now and through 2008. Syrah, Mourvèdre and Grenache.
title: Saxum 2005 James Berry Vineyard Red (Paso Robles)

description: Made with Chianti Classico tradition in mind, this compelling Riserva has measured tones of blueberry, cherry, almond paste and root beer. It exhibits medium-intensity notes of fresh forest fruit on the close. It's simple but extremely genuine overall.
title: Volpaia 2005 Riserva Coltassala  (Chianti Classico)

description: Prune and metallic aromas are not fully integrated and fresh but improve with airing. The palate is heavy and tannic, a touch abrasive in feel, with big berry flavors that hammer away due to fierce tannins. Comes on big with berry fruit but always feels rough.
title: Pizzato 2005 DNA 99 Single Vineyard Merlot (Vale dos Vinhedos)

description: The concentration that Christian Roche produces here is immense, a wine that has dark tannins, the most bitter of chocolate flavors and peppered cherry fruits. Even after four years, this is nowhere open yet, the tannins and the heavy toast wood still dark and dry. Age for 3–4 years more.
title: Domaine l'Ancienne Cure 2005 L'Extase Red (Côtes de Bergerac)

description: This is a mature and toasty vintage, showing notes of yeast, croissant and toast flavors. The fruit is still there, with notes of quince, pear and green plum partnered with acidity. It is balanced, ripe and ready to drink now.
title: Pannier 2005 Vintage Brut  (Champagne)

description: Serious and huge, but talk about a ton of oak: this has a ton and a half, yielding sawdust-based aromas along with creamy plum notes. The thing is, once you realize that you're all-in with the wood, it tastes good, feels good and finishes good. A 3M wine: mint, molasses and medicine. But in this style it's done well.
title: Bodegas Ateca 2005 Atteca Armas Old Vines Garnacha (Calatayud)

description: Olive and espresso notes are backed by bright cherry fruit in this medium-bodied Syrah. It's a pleasant quaff that turns slightly dry on the finish; try it after 2011.
title: Paul Jaboulet Aîné 2005 Le Grand Pompée  (Saint-Joseph)

description: Soft and simple, but the price makes it a pretty good value. Shows cherry, violet, cocoa and smoky oak flavors in a smooth package that finishes dry and spicy.
title: Mandolin 2005 Merlot (Central Coast)

description: Really nice and lush, with an aromatic mix of tobacco, baked fruit, mocha, vanilla, shoe polish, earth and more. It displays a ripe, friendly feel and yummy flavors of mocha, chocolate and savory, subtle berry fruits. Layered on the finish, with lasting notes of fig, mocha and blackberry. Drink now through 2012.
title: Alidis 2005 Expression  (Ribera del Duero)

description: The main aspect that sets Biondi-Santi apart is the delicate nature of its aromas. This is an elegant and feminine Brunello with bright berry nuances, floral notes, wild cherry and cola tones. There are subtle hints of vanilla and spice in the mouth with bright acidity. The 2005 vintage should hold another 10–15 years.
title: Biondi Santi 2005  Brunello di Montalcino

description: Here's a solid everyday Zin, lusty and rustic, offering up a big gulp of fruity flavor. Blackberries, cherries, mulberries, root beer, licorice, and just about every spice you can name shows up. It's a dry, tannic wine that will love rich, fatty meats and cheeses.
title: Renwood 2005 Zinfandel (Sierra Foothills)

description: Comes down on the rustic side, but for the price it's pretty good for a dry Cabernet. Shows the variety's full-bodied fanciness in the blackberry, currant, cocoa and licorice flavors, with a rich steak of vanilla oak.
title: Meridian 2005 Cabernet Sauvignon (California)

description: Superb at every checkpoint, this is a world-class wine. Cherry, cassis and earth aromas are spicy and mellow, not bullish or loud. This feels creamy but with enough textural grit to keep things lively, while flavors of vanilla, tobacco and plum are nicely integrated. A mild, medium-long finish epitomizes elegance. Drink now through 2025.
title: Marqués de Murrieta 2005 Castillo Ygay Gran Reserva Especial  (Rioja)

description: This feminine, delicate rosé sparkling wine from northern Italy opens with a beautiful peachy-pink color and lively aromas of stone fruit, cranberry, pink rose, dried grass and crushed mineral. Pair this wine with caviar or smoked salmon.
title: Bellavista 2005 Gran Cuvée Rosé Sparkling (Franciacorta)

description: Bring on the pizza, pasta and barbecue. Here's a good everyday red at a fair price. It's dry and sturdy in tannins, with berry, cherry, tobacco and spice flavors.
title: St. Francis 2005 Red (Sonoma County)

description: Takes time to unfold after an early blast of saucy spice, hoisin, chili powder and black fruit. The palate is forward and charged, with deep raspberry, cherry and sizzle. Typical of Priorat, there's a gritty ruggedness to the mouthfeel and heat on the finish, but it's by no means rocky. Best through 2011. A Europvin, Christopher Cannan selection, imported by multiple importers, including Michael Skurnik Wines.
title: Clos Figueras 2005 Font de la Figuera Red (Priorat)

description: Full-bodied and robust, this has the tannicly rustic nature of an old-fashioned red. It is completely dry, with earthy flavors of dark stone fruits, currants, coffee, licorice, herbs and pepper.
title: Q 2005 Zinfandel (Sonoma County)

description: Smells and tastes like inexpensive Port, with caramelized wood, baked cherry and sugared black currant jam notes.
title: Rezonja 2005 Cabernet Sauvignon (Alexander Valley)

description: Earthy berry aromas convey quality, and there's oak, floral aromas and tobacco notes for support. Feels dry and tight, with lightly herbal, lightly burnt flavors of plum, berry, tomato and toasted oak. Finishes dry and herbal.
title: Bodegas Bilbainas 2005 Viña Pomal Reserva  (Rioja)

description: The Indian Wells Cabernet Sauvignon seems a little reductive at the moment; odd, since the 2005 vintage was forward, almost fat, and the wine has been in bottle for quite some time. It should definitely be decanted and aired out before serving, which will help to push the fruit flavors forward. The finish is just slightly dilute, but the wine is well-made and may just be suffering from some of the occasional dumbness that strikes Cabernet.
title: Chateau Ste. Michelle 2005 Indian Wells Cabernet Sauvignon (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: Woodsy and smooth smelling, with integrated spice and black-fruit aromas. It feels like a padded vise grip, with demanding tannins and blackberry, toast and coffee flavors that narrow down due to tannic astringency. Incredibly flavorful and potent; drink or hold.
designation: Summa Varietalis Estate Bottled Syrah+Cabernet Sauvignon+Petit Verdot
title: Marques de Griñon 2005 Summa Varietalis Estate Bottled Syrah+Cabernet Sauvignon+Petit Verdot Red (Dominio de Valdepusa)

description: Has a sweaty aroma that's that's hard to get past. In the mouth, the wine is simple, sweet and sharp, with raspberry-mint jelly flavors.
title: Brutocao 2005 Feliz Vineyard Barbera (Mendocino)

description: The estate Syrah immediately shows a toastier nose and richer fruit than the excellent Columbia Valley bottling. Along with cassis, black cherry and blackberry fruit are powerful layers of toasted nuts, butterscotch and coffee. At the moment the wine seems still to be holding something back; decanting and substantial breathing time are highly recommended.
title: Novelty Hill 2005 Stillwater Creek Vineyard Syrah (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: Starts off a bit leathery and lacking fruit, but somewhere along the way raspberries start folding into the otherwise savory mix of leather and black olives. Medium in body, turning tart and tea-like on the finish. Drink now-2012.
title: D'Arenberg 2005 d'Arry's Original Shiraz-Grenache (McLaren Vale)

description: Early on it displays mild heat and jamminess, but with airing everything settles, and ultimately this old-vines Tempranillo from the lauded winemaker Mariano Garcia excels. The palate is rich, minerally and meaty, with slightly syrupy black fruit flavors, molasses and chocolate. Chewy, tannic and pure on the finish, with hints of herbs and lemon peel. Drink now through 2013.
region_1: Vino de la Tierra de Castilla y León
title: Bodegas Leda 2005 Leda Viñas Viejas Tempranillo (Vino de la Tierra de Castilla y León)

description: A shade less opulent but finer and more structured than the '04. The tannins are as fine as in any Napa cult Cab, yet the flavors are exotic, with tiers of black currants, red cherries, licorice, dark chocolate, violets, pepper, leather and Chinese mu shu plum sauce. Drink now with substantial roasts; should age for a decade.
title: C. Beck 2005 Petite Sirah (Napa Valley)

description: A little rubbery and tough on first sniff, but this pedigreed Chilean Cab settles with airing and soon shows exciting, fully ripe raspberry, cassis and blackberry flavors. The mouthfeel is perfectly cushioned yet intense, and the finish is ripe, smooth and composed. Ready to go now but should hold steady for another three years.
title: Concha y Toro 2005 Terrunyo Block: Las Terrazas Cabernet Sauvignon (Pirque)

description: Very dry and fairly soft, with licorice, cherry, cranberry, cola and oak flavors, it lacks the power and complexity you expect from this great property.
title: Clos Pepe 2005 Pinot Noir (Sta. Rita Hills)

description: Underbrush, dried plum, resin, leather and a whiff of baking spice aromas slowly emerge in the glass. The big chewy palate shows juicy blackberry, ripe black cherry, tobacco and clove set against a backbone of firm velvety tannins. Despite its heft, it also boasts surprising finesse. Hold for even more complexity. Drink 2017–2025.
title: Venturini Massimino 2005 Riserva  (Amarone della Valpolicella Classico)

description: Counoise—a southern Rhône blending grape—only rarely appears as a varietal wine, but this distinctive bottling makes a strong case for it. It's not just another generic red wine; it has a particular mouthfeel—elegant, almost Pinot-like—coupled with firm, slightly metallic tannins, sharp acids and accents of pepper on stone. Somehow the wine feels, more than tastes, European.
title: Andrew Rich 2005 Counoise (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: This is one of our favorite red wines from Southern Italy. The reason is personality: you just don't find this intensity and these lead pencil mineral notes and crushed black pepper anywhere else. The wine is elegant and focused with young tannins that promise a long aging future ahead.
title: Mastroberardino 2005 Radici  (Taurasi)

description: La Vis's Dipinti series has preformed well thanks to attractive pricing. This expression of Sauvignon offers evident herbal characteristics, as well as mineral ones. Think chopped sage and basil and drying white stone.
title: La Vis 2005 Dipinti Sauvignon (Trentino)

description: This brings a lot of flavor to the table for its relatively modest price. Round and spicy, it leads with juicy strawberry, cherry and apple pie fruit flavors. Hints of cinnamon wind through the finish, and overall the impression is of a pretty, lively and slightly sweet wine without pretense.
title: Château Bianca 2005 Cellar Select Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

description: Good and well-structured, but sweet, with cherry, blackberry, chocolate and anise flavors that taste like they were baked into a pastry.
title: Leonesse Cellars 2005 Signature Selection Merlot (Temecula Valley)

description: Dry and harsh, almost old-fashioned in its tannic astringency, with a green kick of mint. Relieved by a streak of black cherries and cedar. Drink now.
title: Saddleback 2005 Merlot (Napa Valley)

description: Although the varietal composition isn't typical of Châteauneuf (one-third each Grenache, Syrah, Mourvèdre), the flavors certainly are, combining baked fruit with dusty earth and touches of leather and spice. It's soft and round in the mouth, with a juicy, mouthwatering finish. Drink now-2015.
title: Jean-Luc Colombo 2005 Les Bartavelles Red (Châteauneuf-du-Pape)

description: Blueberry, pepper and cedar aromas and flavors dominate in this structured, elegant red. The wine finishes long with spice and dark cherry notes. A collectible wine that will pair well with poultry, game.
title: Ktima Driopi 2005 Reserve Agiorgitiko (Arcadia)

description: Smooth, wood-polished wine, packed with a ripe, comfortable texture, very ripe red fruits, highlights of tannins. There are black figs, balanced with sweet acidity and fattened with some bacon flavors.
title: J. Portugal Ramos 2005 Aragonês (Alentejano)

description: Grifalco offers a light and elegant style with loads of spice and black fruit flavors. The wine is easy and approachable overall with fresh fruit notes that chime in on the close.
title: Lucania 2005 Grifalco  (Aglianico del Vulture)

description: This dramatically structured Cab needs time in the cellar. It straddles the old-fashioned style of long-aging and the modern style of immediate opulence. The tannins star, sticky and firm, but the wine, which contains minor amounts of Merlot and Cabernet Franc, is so rich, so loaded in black currants, cherries, sweet ripe plums, chocolate, anise and smoky oak, that it seduces now. But best after the age of six.
title: La Sirena 2005 Cabernet Sauvignon (Napa Valley)

description: Here's a firm young Cab that desperately needs time in the cellar. Its tannins push and shove the palate, stinging and shutting it down. But there's a deep core of terrifically ripe black currants, and the oak application is fancy. Only 150 cases produced. Best after 2011 and possibly for many years after.
title: Emilio's Terrace 2005 Sophie's Rows Cabernet Sauvignon (Oakville)

description: Young and unintegrated, this Cab is nowhere near ready to drink. The sweet oak sticks out, overpowering the ripe blackberry, currant and herb flavors. Needs time. Give it until after 2009, at the earliest.
title: Corley 2005 Reserve Cabernet Sauvignon (Napa Valley)

description: One of the most famous Crozes-Hermitage bottlings, the 2005 Domaine de Thalabert is a little tough and unyielding right now, but shows promise in its persistent, peppery finish. It starts off with gamy, leathery notes and hickory smoke, all backed by crisp blackberry fruit and ample tannin. Try after 2012.
title: Paul Jaboulet Aîné 2005 Domaine de Thalabert  (Crozes-Hermitage)

description: Sugary-sweet and Porty, with high acidity and flavors of candied blackberries and cherries.
title: Watts 2005 Los Robles Vineyard Malbec (Clements Hills)

description: Clos du Val has gone as far as they intend to in order to make a ripe, opulent Cab in the modern Napa cult style. But they remain faithful to their Bordeaux-inspired house style, which is leaner and more tannic than most. The result is a natural cellar candidate, with an official alcohol of only 13.5%. Rich in blackberries and black currants, this wine should easily develop over the next 10–15 years.
title: Clos du Val 2005 Cabernet Sauvignon (Stags Leap District)

description: Young, tight, firm and somewhat acidic, this is one Pinot Noir that needs decanting. Like the other releases from Johan, it is a subtle wine that rewards your attention. Details of fruit, earth, rock and herb emerge slowly as the wine opens, suggesting that a few bottles should be tucked away in the cellar for future reference.
title: Johan Vineyards 2005 Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

description: From the uneven 2005 vintage, this bright Brunello opens with aged aromas of tar, resin, black fruit, cassis and leather. The wine's consistency and persistency are very good considering the difficulties of the year.
title: Torrenera 2005  Brunello di Montalcino

description: Leathery cherry and plum aromas offer a hint of tobacco as well as a burnt, roasted note. This is healthy in feel, with lively, snappy acidity. Flavors of herbs, vanilla, tomato, tobacco and red currant finish wiry and high-toned, with moderate tartness and plenty of length. Drink through 2023.
title: Bodegas Ontañón 2005 Gran Reserva Tempranillo-Graciano  (Rioja)

description: This blend of Corvina, Oseleta and Cabernet Franc delivers deeply etched tones of black fruit marmalade, red apple skin and drying minerals. The wine exhibits a deep ruby color and thick extraction to back up all those intense flavors.
title: Tommasi 2005 Crearo della Conca d'Oro Red (Rosso del Veronese)

description: Vanilla cream, banana, Golden Delicious apple and a rubbery note characterize the intense aromas of this Northern Italian wine that moves over the palate in a pleasing way thanks to its creamy smoothness.
title: Eugenio Collavini 2005 Dei Sassi Cavi Chardonnay (Collio)

description: This is a mix of grapes from Willamette and Umpqua Valley vineyards. Light and earthy, it has a classic style that echoes a village Burgundy, only with the distinctive hint of tomato leaf that typifies less expensive Oregon Pinots. There's tart raspberry fruit as well, whiffs of plum and cherry, and overall it packs a lot of interest into its moderately-priced frame.
title: Kings Ridge 2005 Pinot Noir (Oregon)

description: This Riserva Serraboella Barbaresco from the tricky 2005 vintage shows nice concentration and density backed by generous aromas of smoke, ripe cherry and smoked ham. There's a touch of overripe or candied fruit that only helps add to the overall intensity. In the mouth, this wine is silky and very smooth.
title: Rivetti Massimo 2005 Serraboella Riserva  (Barbaresco)

description: Comes out of the chute showing a funky, rubbery edge, but that blows off with a little time to reveal dark, somewhat earthy notes of cola and blackberry. It's medium-bodied, with good length to the finish, where it picks up enough dusty tannins to give it backbone.
title: Ironberry 2005 Cabernet Shiraz Merlot Red (Western Australia)

description: This is one aggressive varietal wine. The nose is packed with sweaty leather and olive notes, while the palate is rough and tart at the edges. There are notes of cola and burnt coffee on the finish, but by then you may just grow tired of wrestling with it.
title: TerraNoble 2005 Reserva Carmenère (Maule Valley)

description: Light in color with pretty layers of minerals and herbs, this golden-hued Picolit offers background aromas of natural rubber, dried mint and stone fruit. It exhibits a traditional style that puts more emphasis on elegance than it does on extraction. It has fresh, sweet flavors and nice density on the close.
title: Giovanni Dri Il Roncat 2005 Picolit (Colli Orientali del Friuli)

description: This delicate Barbaresco moves over the palate lightly and softly, imparting notes of wild berry, cedar, white truffle and licorice. The intensity builds in the mouth, where the wine gains momentum thanks to its tight, compact structure and its lingering tones of smoke and ash.
title: Tenuta Carretta 2005 Garassino  (Barbaresco)

description: A little raw and chunky, with jammy currant and cassis flavors that are wrapped into firm tannins. The finish is dry and astringent, suggesting modest ageability.
title: Hawkstone 2005 Connoisseur Series Cabernet Sauvignon (Oakville)

description: Kettle Valley Winery, on the Naramata Bench, is a rising star. This gorgeous, seductive and silky wine is the perfect expression of Pinot Noir. This is what good Burgundy tastes like, with length, purity and a discreet voluptuousness.
title: Kettle Valley 2005 Pinot Noir (Okanagan Valley)

description: Aromas of mint, cedar and spices are followed by medium-bodied vanilla and berry flavors in this approachable red from Greece. The dry-style red offers good acidity and balance and will pair well with dishes of the Mediterranean—think olives, grilled lamb, moussaka.
title: Boutari 2005 Xinomavro (Naoussa)

description: The Dierberg family worked hard before releasing this Cab, which comes from the warmest part of the appellation. It's quite a good wine, soft and rich and immediately likeable for its array of black currant, licorice and mocha flavors. The 100% new French oak is just right in smoke and cedary vanilla. The next step is to build in layers of density and complexity, but this is a huge step forward for Santa Ynez Valley Cabernet Sauvignon.
title: Star Lane Vineyard 2005 Cabernet Sauvignon (Santa Ynez Valley)

description: Surh Luchtel knows how to make a great Cab. The '05 is a beautiful wine, softly complex and layered, with black currant, cherry, mocha, sweet oak and spicy flavors. But it's tannins that really mark this wine. They're so fine, so authoritative. Drink now–2010.
title: Surh Luchtel 2005 Cabernet Sauvignon (Napa Valley)

description: A big success, this a wine that manages to combine the ripeness of Merlot fruit from 2005 with impressive tannins and power and still have structure and acidity. This should age well over 10 years.
title: Clos du Clocher 2005  Pomerol

description: Tobacco leaf, new leather, forest floor and blackberry open the nose of this nicely ruby-colored Brunello di Montalcino. This wine delivers aromas of cedar wood, vanilla seed, black fruit and exotic spice. In the mouth, it is bright and tonic with sweet oak or cherry fruit flavors on the close. It's almost ready to drink now.
title: Camigliano 2005  Brunello di Montalcino

description: Just hinting at maturity, this rich and stylish wine features finely balanced tannins, a juicy blackberry flavor and fruity ripeness. Using fruit sourced from the small appellation of Canon-Fronsac, on the hill close to Saint-Émilion, this wine is ready to drink and is full of flavor, with concentration on the finish.
title: Château Canon 2005  Canon-Fronsac

description: Classic Sauvignon, steely, crisp and mineral. There is a concentration of texture and green skins as well as impressive freshness. Lively, vibrant flavors, vivid in their relief, point up the good intensity of this fruity wine.
title: Fournier Père et Fils 2005 La Chaudouillonne  (Sancerre)

description: A dark, brooding style of tannic Douro wine. It is lean initially, with a strong mineral character and firm black plum-skin flavors plus leather and tight acidity. When it opens out, this becomes a great value from the Douro.
title: DFJ Vinhos 2005 Vega Red (Douro)

description: Made in a cool part of Napa Valley in a cool vintage, this Syrah is very dry and tough in youthful tannins. It's too astringent to offer much pleasure now, but has a great heart of ripe blackberry, dark chocolate, leather, tobacco, anise and pepper flavor that should sweeten and emerge as the wine ages. Try holding until after 2009.
title: Moffett 2005 Syrah (Oak Knoll District)

description: Unusually exotic and strange on the bouquet, with blueberry, lemon peel and warm mulch aromas. The palate has size and pretty good feel, with tangy blue-fruit flavors, exotic spice notes and zip. Finishes with floral notes and lightness.
title: Viña Ijalba 2005 Crianza  (Rioja)

description: Dill and butter aromas announce aging in American oak, while the berry aromas are solid but common. This is a tightly wound wine with black cherry and olive flavors followed by length on the palate. It's not edgy, complex or beguiling, but it is full of fruit and has good balance. Imported by Martín Códax USA.
title: Martín Códax 2005 Ergo  (Rioja)

description: Feels almost Porty, with a hot, harsh mouthfeel and flavors of dessicated grapes and grapeskin tannins dominating the blackberries and currants. Could age out, if you're a gambler.
title: Rezonja 2005 Merlot (Alexander Valley)

description: Drier and earthier than many Napa Cabs these days, this wine has subtle sage and sweet licorice that grounds its fruitier flavors of blackberries, cherries and raspberries. Very beautiful are the tannins, which are rich and pure, suggesting perfect ripeness. The small Bosché bottling has been a premier one for the winery for many years, and this '05 is one of the best in memory. Should age effortlessly for at least a dozen years.
title: Freemark Abbey 2005 Bosché Cabernet Sauvignon (Rutherford)

description: Intense and extracted, with vanilla and char aromas upfront and cherry schnapps flavors in the mouth. Smooth tannins suggest a lean meat such as veal cutlets. Drink now to 2011.
title: Christiano Family 2005 Reserve Merlot (North Fork of Long Island)

description: This medium-bodied Pinot features aromas of vanilla, beet greens and a touch of cola to go with extremely soft tannins. Flavors of cola, cedar and earth end on hints of tart berries and coffee.
title: Nepenthe 2005 Charleston Pinot Noir (Adelaide Hills)

description: This is taut and dense, and requires time and air to untangle. Deep, dark fruits are mixed in with layers of black olive, smoke, compost, tar, moist earth and herb—a delicious mélange of flavors that promises good things to come as the wine ages. It's masterful winemaking in a package so young and wrapped up tight that it may pass you by if you don't give it a good look.
title: Woodward Canyon 2005 Artist Series #14 Cabernet Sauvignon (Columbia Valley (WA))
